title: Russia Targets RAGE Receptor in 'Anti-Aging Vaccine,' Aiming for First Gene Therapy Approval
description: Russia's deputy science minister disclosed an experimental gene therapy targeting the RAGE receptor - a key driver of cellular aging and inflammation - with the stated goal of being 'the world's first gene therapy drug' for aging. Production is targeted for 2028-2030 under a 2 trillion ruble ($26.4B) national health program, signaling state-level competition for longevity sovereignty alongside US and Chinese biotech.

title: Bowhead Whale DNA Repair Pathways Reframe Human Cancer-Longevity Strategy
description: Fight Aging's weekend deep-dive on Molecular Oncology data attributes the bowhead whale's >200-year lifespan and near-zero cancer rate to superior genome maintenance. With Peto's paradox closer to a mechanistic answer, the implication is that boosting human DNA repair fidelity may be a more powerful longevity lever than slowing senescence alone.

title: Senolytic Class Selection Now Matters Clinically: D+Q Beats Navitoclax in Disc Trial
description: A new disc-degeneration study in SM/J mice shows Dasatinib + Quercetin reduced senescence markers (p19ARF, p21, SASP) and degeneration severity while Navitoclax did not - the strongest published head-to-head sharpening of the senolytic hierarchy. The result reinforces D+Q as the most clinically translatable senolytic stack as broader human trials accelerate.

title: Cardiac Reprogramming Joins Optic Reprogramming as Second Tissue with Positive In-Vivo Signal
description: Lifespan.io reports partial epigenetic reprogramming softens heart attack damage in mice by helping cardiomyocytes complete division. With Life Biosciences' Phase 1 already running in optic neuropathies, partial reprogramming is now generating positive in-vivo signal in two of the body's most senescence-resistant tissues, expanding the therapeutic surface from rare ophthalmic conditions to ischemic heart disease.

title: Hematopoietic Stem Cell Aging Pinpointed to RIPK3-MLKL Axis - New Drug Target Emerges
description: A Nature Communications study fingers necroptosis machinery (RIPK3-MLKL) as the convergence point through which multiple stress responses drive age-related decline of hematopoietic stem cells - impairing lymphoid differentiation and mitochondrial function. The finding identifies a tractable druggable node for restoring immune competence in older adults and aligns with the inflammaging-statin thesis dominating recent coverage.

title: Russia Develops 'Anti-Aging Vaccine' Targeting Cellular Aging via RAGE
description: Deputy science minister Sekirinsky says the Institute of Aging Biology and Medicine is developing a gene therapy blocking the RAGE receptor, with manufacturing targeted for 2028-2030 under a $26.4B national project.

title: Bowhead Whales' Superior DNA Repair Explains Their Low Cancer Rate
description: Molecular Oncology commentary attributes bowhead whales' exceptional lifespan and near-zero cancer to superior genome maintenance - a candidate target for human anti-cancer and longevity therapy.

title: D+Q Outperforms Navitoclax in Disc-Senescence Mouse Model
description: DQ-treated SM/J mice showed reduced p19ARF/p21/SASP and disc degeneration; Navitoclax did not - JUN inhibition mimics the benefit in human NP cells, suggesting senolytic-class selection matters clinically.

title: Reprogrammed Cardiomyocytes Soften the Blow in Heart Attack
description: Partial reprogramming helps heart muscle cells complete division after MI in mice, mitigating damage and opening cardiac applications for the same toolkit Life Biosciences is testing in human eyes.

title: RIPK3-MLKL Axis Drives Hematopoietic Stem Cell Aging
description: Multiple stress responses converge on RIPK3-MLKL to induce age-related HSC changes, impairing self-renewal and lymphoid differentiation - a new candidate druggable node for restoring immune resilience.

title: Cochrane Meta-Analysis: Amyloid-Beta-Targeting Antibodies Show Trivial Cognitive Effects
description: Meta-analysis of nine Aβ-mAbs (aducanumab, donanemab, lecanemab and others) shows trivial cognitive effects, small functional effects at best, with increased ARIA risk in MCI and mild Alzheimer's - reframing where the field should invest next.

title: Enhancer-Directed Gene Delivery Restores Digit-Tip Regeneration in Mice
description: Conserved SP6/SP8 transcription factors plus FGF8 delivered via AAV vectors partially rescue digit tip regeneration in knockout mice and accelerate it in wild-type mice - a step toward limb-regenerative biology.

title: Gut Microbiota Correlates with Physical Function in Healthy Older Adults
description: Prevotella copri and Roseburia track positively with PA and function; Bilophila wadsworthia and Eggerthella track negatively with PA and grip strength in 101 community-dwelling adults aged 65-85.

title: Chronic Kidney Disease Tightly Bound to Cellular Senescence
description: Reviews highlight a bidirectional loop in which kidney injury promotes senescence and senescent cells fuel further inflammation and fibrosis - candidate target for senolytic precision endotype-based intervention.

title: The 'Skinspan' Concept Pivots Aesthetic Dermatology to Healthspan
description: A new Journal of Cosmetic Dermatology piece argues skin healthspan should be tracked as a longevity endpoint with clinical metrics for barrier, regenerative capacity, and aesthetic quality.

title: GSK Acquires 35Pharma in $950M Deal, Advancing PH Drug
description: GSK adds a clinical-stage candidate for PAH and PH-HFpEF; proof-of-concept trials expected to begin soon, validating cardiopulmonary aging as a strategic healthspan category.

title: Seragon's SRN-901 Extends Median Lifespan in Mice 33%
description: Oral five-component longevity drug extended late-life lifespan 33%, cut frailty 70% and tumor incidence 30% in Western-diet-fed mice, beating rapamycin head-to-head.

title: Energy Span Reframes Fatigue as Early Warning of Aging
description: New framework treats mitochondrial fatigue as a precursor to chronic disease tracked through wearables (HRV, glucose dynamics, sleep architecture), particularly relevant for women.

title: Biggest Longevity Breakthroughs This Week - Week 17 Roundup
description: Phil Newman and Nina Patrick cover energy span, GSK's $950M, January AI on Medicare, OsteoBoost $8M, Seragon SRN-901 33%, and Epicutis TCP serum's 77% skin improvement.

title: OsteoBoost Raises $8M to Scale Bone-Loading Wearable
description: Vibration wearable mimics bone-loading to combat osteoporosis affecting 200M people, offering a non-drug alternative to bisphosphonates.

title: January AI Enters Medicare App Library, Reaches 69M Patients
description: AI-driven food-photo metabolic coaching joins Medicare's reimbursed app library, removing cost barrier for a 69M senior population.

title: FDA Clears First Cellular Rejuvenation Trial - $120B Longevity Market
description: Market commentary spotlights FDA clearance of the first cellular rejuvenation trial and a longevity market on track from $85B in 2025 to ~$120B by 2030.

title: How the Body Really Ages: 7 Million Cells Mapped Across 21 Organs
description: Rockefeller's Cao lab publishes in Science the most detailed atlas yet of aging across 21 mouse tissues, identifying cytokines as systemic intervention candidates.

title: Insilico Forms Industry's First Longevity Board Chaired by Lilly's Andrew Adams
description: Clinical-stage AI biotech Insilico Medicine launches a Longevity Board to oversee AI-driven aging research and dual-purpose drug discovery targeting hallmarks of aging.

title: The Immune System Ages Differently in Men and Women
description: Single-cell RNA study of ~1,000 people identifies sex-divergent immune aging patterns, calling for sex-stratified inflammaging biomarkers.

title: Rapamycin Might Blunt Exercise Response in Humans
description: New human data suggest chronic rapamycin can dampen exercise adaptations in older subjects, raising practical protocol questions for geroprotective dosing.

title: BioAge Reports Positive Phase 1 Data for BGE-102
description: BioAge's NLRP3-inhibitor program for metabolic diseases of aging shows positive Phase 1 tolerability and early activity, supporting later-stage development.

title: Life Biosciences Secures $80M Series D Financing
description: David Sinclair co-founded Life Bio closes $80M to complete Phase 1 ER-100 trial in optic neuropathies and extend its partial epigenetic reprogramming runway.

title: Mitrix Moves Mitochondria into the Clinic
description: Mitrix Bio reports Phase 1 safety with transplanted mitochondria in two older participants and opens Right-to-Try clinics in Newport Beach, Dallas, and Palm Beach.

title: Top Trends from A4M Longevity SpringFest 2026
description: Biological-age testing (GrimAge, DunedinPACE, OmicAge) enters routine clinical care; 'brainspan' emerges as longevity medicine's new cognitive endpoint.

title: David Sinclair Backs NAD+ Plus CD38 Inhibition Longevity Stack
description: Sinclair endorses a dual approach - boosting NAD+ precursors while inhibiting NAD-consuming CD38 - as a next-gen geroprotective stack.

title: Bryan Johnson's Blueprint Protocol 2026 Update
description: 2026 refinements emphasize organ-specific metrics and frequent labs, reduce NMN/NR frequency, and add low-dose lithium and NDGA.

title: Tozorakimab Meets Primary Endpoint in Phase III MIRANDA COPD Trial
description: AstraZeneca's anti-IL-33 tozorakimab hit its primary endpoint in MIRANDA, supporting a key inflammaging pathway in age-related COPD.

title: Longevity Biotech Investment 2026: Set for a Breakout Year
description: DLT Chatbot estimates full-year 2026 longevity funding of $8-9B (55-60% above 2025's $5.72B), with Q1 at $3.74B across 49 deals.
